steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills the verb in a that-clause: "that the Continental Army's difficulty in procuring supplies... ______ due to concerns..." The subject of the blank is "difficulty"—a singular noun. We need a finite verb (not just a participle) that agrees with a singular subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — largely occur: Plural verb form; does not agree with the singular subject "difficulty." ✗
  • Choice B — largely occurring: A bare participle with no auxiliary; cannot serve as the finite main verb of the clause. ✗
  • Choice C — is largely occurring: Singular auxiliary "is" correctly agrees with "difficulty," and the present progressive signals the ongoing situation Witherspoon is describing. ✓
  • Choice D — have largely occurred: Plural auxiliary "have" does not agree with the singular subject "difficulty." ✗
C is correct.
